* Refactor TracingHelper class → module functions + host method

Replaced the TracingHelper class with:
- configure_tracing() — standalone function for exporter setup,
  overridable via AgentServerHost(configure_tracing=my_func) or
  disabled with configure_tracing=None
- request_span() — module-level context manager for span creation
- end_span/record_error/trace_stream — module-level lifecycle helpers
- AgentServerHost.request_span() — thin method that delegates with
  pre-populated host identity (agent_id, project_id)

self._tracing.request_span() with None checks. All functions are
no-ops when opentelemetry-api is not installed.

* fix(core): add flush_spans() to drain BatchSpanProcessor before sandbox suspend (#46181)

BatchSpanProcessor exports spans on a background timer (default 5s).
In hosted sandbox environments the platform may suspend the process
immediately after an HTTP response is sent, before the timer fires.
This causes short-lived spans (e.g. LangGraph per-node invoke_agent
spans) to be lost.

Add flush_spans() to the core public API and call it from
trace_stream's finally block so the streaming path also flushes.

Verified: same agent produces 19-31 spans with flush vs 11 without.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Docker build — copy full pnpm workspace deps instead of individual node_modules

The COPY instruction doesn't support shell operators (2>/dev/null || true),
so copying non-existent packages/shared/node_modules caused a hard failure.

Fix: copy entire dep tree from deps stage with 'COPY --from=deps /app ./'
which preserves pnpm's hoisted workspace structure correctly.

Also adds docker-compose.web-only.yml for frontend-only deployment.

* fix(retry): retry InvalidRequest when caused by malformed JSON from model output

InvalidRequest was unconditionally non-retryable, but errors like
"can't find closing '}'" or "unexpected character in JSON" are transient —
they stem from the model generating invalid tool_call JSON that
llama-server rejected. A fresh prompt may produce valid output.

Add malformed_json_indicators substring list and match against the error
message to selectively retry these cases. Add 6 inline tests covering
matching and non-matching scenarios.

fix: pre-clean Python 3.14 MSI leftovers before installing

Previous failed attempts leave JustForMe package registrations in the
MSI database. When the bundle migrates them to AllUsers, it uninstalls
the JustForMe packages AFTER installing AllUsers ones - both to the same
TargetDir - wiping the stdlib and causing 'Could not find platform
independent libraries' on first python.exe run.

fresh install so the bundle sees a clean slate.

Validate version on release (#22)

Fixes https://github.com/github/spark/issues/2259

Our typical release process for this component is just to set a tagged
release and let that go. This has the downside that our binary also
thinks it knows its version over in cmd/version.go.

Rather than do something elaborate when we're not releasing this often,
this PR simply adds a guardrail that will fail the release if the
versions don't align. In that case, the move is to remove the partial
release/tag, commit the updated version, and then release.

This should be sufficient for the moment, and if we get working on this
more often to where it's an annoyance, we can put a more thorough system
in place.

commit.message:
feat(palette-builder): add actionable accessibility fix suggestions in theme arranger

When a text/bg or accent/bg pairing fails WCAG contrast thresholds, the
theme arranger now shows an actionable suggestion instead of a text hint:
- A color swatch showing the suggested fix color
- The exact hex value
- A label describing the fix (e.g. 'lighter text', 'darker accent')
- An 'apply' button that immediately swaps the color into that role

findAccessibleVariant() steps a color's HSL lightness until it meets the
target ratio (7:1 for AAA text, 4.5:1 for AA). For dark mode it tries
lightening the foreground first, then darkening the background as fallback.
For light mode the directions are reversed.

refactor: simplify fact-check to 3 layers (regex+LLM+test)

Remove fragile regex heuristics (factCheckNumbers, assertNoFabricatedStatistics)
that kept causing false positives. Promote llmFactCheck to primary blocking gate
with multi-model fallback, structured verification prompt covering all 7 categories
(laws, institutions, rates, statistics, dates, source coherence, invented facts),
lower blocking threshold (0.6), and fail-safe when all verification models unavailable.

Architecture: L1=assertNoFabricatedReferences (fast regex pre-filter) →
L2=llmFactCheck (primary gate, GPT-4.1→GPT-4o→Gemini Flash fallback) →

commit.message:
fix(eoc-crawler): prevent slug churn from location instability

EOC jobs accumulated 10+ previousSlugs each (958 total), bloating the
build with thousands of redirect pages. Root causes:

1. Location instability: extractEocLocation() produces different cities
   across crawl runs (Bellinzona→Novaggio→Lugano) depending on which
   hospital name appears first in the description. The base crawler's
   merge step treats location changes as genuine slug changes.

2. No cap on previousSlugs: every slug change accumulated indefinitely.

Fixes:
- Add stabilizeEocSlugsInMemory(): compares post-merge jobs with
  pre-crawl slice data by vacancy ID. If the title is unchanged, any
  slug change is location-driven and gets reverted.
- Add previousSlugs capping (MAX_PREVIOUS_SLUGS=5) in
  postProcessEocJobsInMemory(): deduplicates against current slugs,
  then keeps only the 5 most recent entries.
- Cap existing data: 958→660 previousSlugs (31% reduction).

Tests: 12 tests pass (5 new covering stabilization + capping).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: fix finality-lock busy-loop with proper cooldown field

- Add pub fork_resolution_blocked_until: Arc<AtomicU64> to Blockchain
  struct (Unix-second deadline). Replaces the previous attempt that
  wrote same_height_fork_cooldown with our own hash instead of the
  peer's hash (so it never matched the check in compare_chain_with_peers
  and the cooldown had no effect).

- Set fork_resolution_blocked_until = now+120s whenever the finality
  lock fires and blocks a reorg.  This prevents the full deep-fetch
  cycle from being restarted every ~15 s (one ping interval) before
  the 60 s longer_chain_escape timer can accumulate.

- Guard handle_fork() entry: if cooldown is active and the call is
  NOT a continuation of an already-active FetchingChain for this peer,
  return early with a debug log.

- Extend the ping-handler GetBlocks guard in message_handler.rs to
  also check fork_resolution_blocked_until, so the cooldown is
  respected from both spawn paths (handle_fork + ping handler).

- Also added in prior edits (same session):
    * MIN_PEERS_FINALITY_OVERRIDE: 5 → 2
    * longer_chain_escape: bypass finality lock after 60s if even 1
      peer is strictly ahead and majority of ahead-peers agree

* Extracted tryAppendStaticMemberValueFromTypeName()

* Partial fix for template default materialization substitution

1. SFINAE: reject a function template instantiation if the return type is still an unresolved ::type placeholder after dependent-alias resolution.
2. integral_constant compile failures: let the existing IR fallback perform the proven-safe int -> bool variable-init conversion instead of aborting on the missing sema
annotation.

* Fixed template instatiation arguments not taking dependency state into account

The root cause was that dependent non-type template args like B were collapsing onto concrete value args like false in template-instantiation identity, so integral_constant<bool, B> and integral_constant<bool, false> reused the same placeholder path. I fixed that in the template argument/key machinery (src\TemplateRegistry_Types.h, src\TemplateTypes.h) and kept the alias materialization path consistent in the parser/instantiation flow (src\Parser_Decl_TopLevel.cpp, src\Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp, src\Parser_Templates_Inst_ClassTemplate.cpp, src\Parser_Templates_Inst_Substitution.cpp, src\Parser_TypeSpecifiers.cpp)
